heading: Recovery of expenditures under certain conditions
label: 291i Recovery of expenditures under certain conditions

Amendments1984— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “If any facility with respect to which funds have been paid under shall, at any time within twenty years after the completion of construction—Pub. L. 98–369section 291f of this title“(a) be sold or transferred to any person, agency, or organization (1) which is not qualified to file an application under , or (2) which is not approved as a transferee by the State agency designated pursuant to , or its successor, orsection 291e of this titlesection 291d of this title“(b) cease to be a public health center or a public or other nonprofit hospital, outpatient facility, facility for long-term care, or rehabilitation facility, unless the Surgeon General determines, in accordance with regulations, that there is good cause for releasing the applicant or other owner from this obligation,the United States shall be entitled to recover from either the transferor or the transferee (or, in the case of a facility which has ceased to be public or nonprofit, from the owners thereof) an amount bearing the same ratio to the then value (as determined by the agreement of the parties or by action brought in the district court of the United States for the district in which the facility is situated) of so much of the facility as constituted an approved project or projects, as the amount of the Federal participation bore to the cost of the construction or modernization under such project or projects. Such right of recovery shall not constitute a lien upon said facility prior to judgment.”1970—Cl. (b). substituted “outpatient facility” for “diagnostic or treatment center”.Pub. L. 91–296

Change of Name“Secretary of Health and Human Services” substituted for “Secretary of Health, Education, and Welfare” in subsecs. (b), (c), and (d) pursuant to , which is classified to , Education.section 509(b) of Pub. L. 96–88section 3508(b) of Title 20

children.text: be sold or transferred to any entity (A) which is not qualified to file an application under , or (B) which is not approved as a transferee by the State agency designated pursuant to , or its successor, orsection 291e of this titlesection 291d of this title
cease to be a public health center or a public or other nonprofit hospital, outpatient facility, facility for long-term care, or rehabilitation facility,

children.heading: Notice to Secretary
children.label: b Notice to Secretary
children.text: The transferor of a facility which is sold or transferred as described in subsection (a)(1), or the owner of a facility the use of which is changed as described in subsection (a)(2), shall provide the Secretary written notice of such sale, transfer, or change not later than the expiration of 10 days from the date on which such sale, transfer, or change occurs.

children.heading: Amount of recovery; interest; interest period
children.label: c Amount of recovery; interest; interest period

children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the amount the United States shall be entitled to recover under subsection (a) is an amount bearing the same ratio to the then value (as determined by the agreement of the parties or in an action brought in the district court of the United States for the district for which the facility involved is situated) of so much of the facility as constituted an approved project or projects as the amount of the Federal participation bore to the cost of the construction or modernization of such project or projects.

children.label: i
children.text: 180 days after the date of the sale, transfer, or change of use for which a notice is required by subsection (b), in the case of a facility which is sold or transferred or the use of which changes after , orJuly 18, 1984

children.label: ii
children.text: thirty days after , or if later 180 days after the date of the sale, transfer, or change of use for which a notice is required by subsection (b), in the case of a facility which was sold or transferred or the use of which changed before .July 18, 1984July 18, 19841So in original. The period probably should be a comma.1

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a facility which was sold or transferred or the use of which changed before , thirty days after such date or if later 180 days after the date of the sale, transfer, or change of use for which a notice is required by subsection (b),July 18, 1984

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a facility with respect to which notice is provided in accordance with subsection (b), upon the expiration of 180 days after the receipt of such notice, or

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a facility with respect to which such notice is not provided as prescribed by subsection (b), on the date of the sale, transfer, or change of use for which such notice was to be provided,

children.label: i
children.text: in an amount equal to the greater of twice the cost of the remaining obligation of the facility under clause (2) of or the amount, determined under subsection (c), that the United States is entitled to recover, andsection 291c(e) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: which will only be used by the entity to provide the care required by clause (2) of ; andsection 291c(e) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: will meet the obligation of the facility under clause (1) of .section 291c(e) of this title

The Secretary may waive the recovery rights of the United States under subsection (a)(2) with respect to a facility in any State if the Secretary determines, in accordance with regulations, that there is good cause for waiving such rights with respect to such facility.

children.heading: Lien
children.label: e Lien
children.text: The right of recovery of the United States under subsection (a) shall not constitute a lien on any facility with respect to which funds have been paid under .section 291f of this title
